text.title: ZDR fallback — encrypted reasoning content
text.summary: Under store=false, previous_response_id is silently ignored; round-trip include=["reasoning.encrypted_content"] yourself.

rule.statement: When the request has store=false (Zero Data Retention), set include=["reasoning.encrypted_content"] on every Responses call and append the encrypted reasoning items from the prior response into the next call's input array; do not pass previous_response_id — it is ignored without a server-side store.
rule.rationale: ZDR contractually disallows server-side persistence, so the API has nothing to look up by id. The encrypted-content opt-in returns reasoning items as opaque blobs that the next call decrypts in-process only — preserving reasoning continuity without retaining anything at rest.

example.note: Encrypted blobs are passed back literally; the server decrypts in-memory and reattaches to the new turn.

section.title: Cost note
section.p: Encrypted reasoning items are larger than their unencrypted counterparts. Expect ~5-15% input-token overhead vs the stored variant.

antipattern.description: Using encrypted-content round-trip when the deployment does NOT have a ZDR requirement.
antipattern.reason: Pays the encryption tax for no compliance benefit; previous_response_id is cheaper and equivalent for stored deployments.

antipattern.description: Passing previous_response_id with store=false and assuming reasoning carries forward.
antipattern.reason: The id resolves to nothing because nothing was stored; the call succeeds but reasoning is lost — silent regression on quality.

section.reference: OpenAI community thread — encrypted_content with store=false.
